parameters.content:
## Code Review ReAct Agent — GitHub PR mode
Keeps the Solution-Kit ReAct agent + 5 deterministic tools, and adds the
"Connect to pull requests" future-work item.

**Flow**
GitHub Trigger (pull_request) -> Filter PR Events -> Fetch Diff -> Parse Input -> ReAct Agent (+tools) -> Format Output -> Create a review.

**Setup**
1. GitHub OAuth2 cred on: GitHub Trigger, Fetch Diff, Create a review.
2. OpenAI cred on: OpenAI Chat Model (gpt-4o-mini, temp 0.2 per kit).
3. Activate (do NOT use listen-mode — an active workflow registers the real webhook).

Review is posted with event=comment (non-gating). Change in Create a review to gate merges.

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert code review agent that follows the ReAct (Reasoning + Acting) pattern. You are reviewing a GitHub PULL REQUEST provided as a unified diff. Lines starting with '+' are added, '-' are removed, and lines beginning with '@@' are hunk headers. Focus your judgment on the added/changed lines.

Tools:
1. get_code - Returns code, declared language, and context. Call FIRST.
2. ast_complexity_analysis - Structure, complexity, and performance clues.
3. security_analysis - Deterministic security pattern matching.
4. quality_regex_analysis - Code quality and style checks.
5. web_search - Public best practices, CVEs, or language-specific idioms. Use sparingly.

For every step, reason explicitly:
Thought: what do I need to know next, and why?
Action: which tool to call, or Final Answer when done.
Observation: what the tool returned.

Always call get_code first, then gather objective evidence from the deterministic
tools before forming any opinion. Only use web_search when external context is genuinely
needed. Tie every serious finding to evidence from the tools. The deterministic tools see
raw diff text (including +/- prefixes), so treat their line numbers as approximate.

Cover four dimensions:
1. Code Quality
2. Security
3. Performance
4. Maintainability

When you have enough evidence, respond with ONLY the final markdown report in EXACTLY this format:

# Code Review Report

## Overall Grade: A | B | C | D | F

## Executive Summary
(2-3 sentences. State the verdict and the most important issue.)

## Critical Issues (must fix)
- ...

## Warnings (should fix)
- ...

## Suggestions (nice to have)
- ...

## Strengths
- ...

## Dimension Scores
| Dimension | Score | One-line rationale |
| Quality | X/10 | ... |
| Security | X/10 | ... |
| Performance | X/10 | ... |
| Maintainability | X/10 | ... |

## Priority Action Items
1. [CRITICAL] ...
2. [HIGH] ...
3. [MEDIUM] ...

parameters.name: get_code
parameters.description: Returns the submitted diff, declared language, and PR context. Call this FIRST. Input is ignored.

parameters.name: ast_complexity_analysis
parameters.description: Heuristic structure/complexity/performance analysis: line counts, function count, nested loops, sequential awaits in loops, possible N+1 queries, chained array passes. Input is ignored; it reads the submitted code directly.

parameters.name: security_analysis
parameters.description: Deterministic security pattern scan: eval/dynamic exec, shell/process spawning, hardcoded secrets, SQL string interpolation, DOM injection, weak hashes, insecure randomness, disabled TLS verification. Input is ignored.

parameters.name: quality_regex_analysis
parameters.description: Code quality/style scan: var usage, console/print logging, TODO/FIXME, loose equality, swallowed errors, low comment ratio. Input is ignored.

parameters.toolDescription: Search the public web (DuckDuckGo instant-answer API) for best practices, CVEs, or language-specific idioms. Use sparingly and only when external context is needed.

values.name: searchTerm
values.description: The search query (e.g. a CVE id, library name, or best-practice topic).

parameters.body:
=## AI Code Review

{{ $json.report }}

---
_Reviewed commit `{{ $('Filter PR Events').item.json.pull_request.head.sha }}` • tools used: {{ (($json.metadata && $json.metadata.toolsUsed) || []).join(', ') || 'none' }}_
